title: FastAPI for ML Inference Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of building production-grade ML APIs with FastAPI.

questions.question: Where should you load an ML model (e.g., with joblib.load()) in a FastAPI application?

options.text: Inside the prediction route handler, so it gets the latest version on every request
In the application lifespan startup event, loaded once into memory and reused
In a background task triggered by the first request
In a separate thread that re-loads the model every 60 seconds

questions.question: A Pydantic BaseModel in FastAPI's route definition primarily serves which purpose?

options.text: To serialize the model weights to disk
To automatically validate and type-coerce the incoming request body, returning a 422 on invalid input
To connect the API to the database via an ORM
To define the Kubernetes deployment manifest

questions.question: Your model takes 8 seconds to produce a prediction. What is the best API design?

options.text: Increase the client timeout to 30 seconds so it can wait
Use synchronous processing and hope the network doesn't drop
Accept the request, return a job ID, process the prediction asynchronously, and expose a status endpoint
Split the model into 8 smaller models that each take 1 second

questions.question: What does the FastAPI middleware decorator `@app.middleware('http')` allow you to do?

options.text: Lock an endpoint to only accept POST requests
Intercept every request and response to add logging, timing, or CORS headers
Define a background job that runs on a schedule
Register a new database model with the ORM

questions.question: What is the recommended production command to run a FastAPI app with multiple workers?

options.text: python main.py
uvicorn main:app --reload
gunicorn -w 4 -k uvicorn.workers.UvicornWorker app.main:app
flask run --host 0.0.0.0
